question: Tell me Daily/weekly/Monthly summary for DT meter (DT meter ID) for today/yesterday/Week period/Month period. This query should include summaries from Interval data, Event data. Summaries like Peak Load, Avg Load with time or time range, Event with severity and category, counts and minutes for which event was there.
sql:
WITH params AS (
    SELECT 'dev__EEOT4056946' AS dt_meter_id, 'THIS_WEEK' AS period_type
),
event_master (event_code, description, severity, category, direction, pair_code) AS (
    VALUES
    ('0003','Y-Phase VT link missing (Missing potential)','HIGH',  'Grid Reliability',   'OCCURRENCE','0004'),
    ('0004','Y-Phase VT link missing (Missing potential)','MEDIUM','Grid Reliability',   'RESTORATION','0003'),
    ('0009','Low voltage in any phase',                   'HIGH',  'Grid Reliability',   'OCCURRENCE','0010'),
    ('0010','Low voltage in any phase',                   'MEDIUM','Grid Reliability',   'RESTORATION','0009'),
    ('0051','R Phase Current reverse',                    'HIGH',  'Revenue Protection', 'OCCURRENCE','0052'),
    ('0052','R Phase Current reverse',                    'MEDIUM','Revenue Protection', 'RESTORATION','0051'),
    ('0063','Current unbalance',                          'HIGH',  'Network Health',     'OCCURRENCE','0064'),
    ('0064','Current unbalance',                          'MEDIUM','Network Health',     'RESTORATION','0063'),
    ('0101','Power Failure (3 Phase)',                    'HIGH',  'Grid Reliability',   'OCCURRENCE','0102'),
    ('0102','Power Failure (3 Phase)',                    'MEDIUM','Grid Reliability',   'RESTORATION','0101'),
    ('0161','LLS secret (MR) Change',                     'HIGH',  'Security',           'STANDALONE', NULL),
    ('0162','HLS key (US) change',                        'HIGH',  'Security',           'STANDALONE', NULL),
    ('0163','HLS key (FW) change',                        'HIGH',  'Security',           'STANDALONE', NULL),
    ('0205','Very Low PF',                                'HIGH',  'Network Health',     'OCCURRENCE','0206'),
    ('0206','Very Low PF',                                'MEDIUM','Network Health',     'RESTORATION','0205'),
    ('0209','Plug in Communication Module Removal',       'HIGH',  'Security',           'OCCURRENCE','0210'),
    ('0210','Plug in Communication Module Removal',       'MEDIUM','Security',           'RESTORATION','0209'),
    ('0301','Meter Load disconnected',                    'HIGH',  'Operational Control','OCCURRENCE','0302'),
    ('0302','Meter Load connected',                       'MEDIUM','Operational Control','RESTORATION','0301')
),
period AS (
    SELECT
        CASE (SELECT period_type FROM params)
            WHEN 'TODAY'      THEN CURRENT_DATE::timestamp
            WHEN 'YESTERDAY'  THEN (CURRENT_DATE - 1)::timestamp
            WHEN 'THIS_WEEK'  THEN date_trunc('week',  CURRENT_DATE)::timestamp
            WHEN 'LAST_WEEK'  THEN (date_trunc('week', CURRENT_DATE) - INTERVAL '7 days')::timestamp
            WHEN 'THIS_MONTH' THEN date_trunc('month', CURRENT_DATE)::timestamp
            WHEN 'LAST_MONTH' THEN (date_trunc('month',CURRENT_DATE) - INTERVAL '1 month')::timestamp
        END AS start_ts,
        CASE (SELECT period_type FROM params)
            WHEN 'TODAY'      THEN (CURRENT_DATE + 1)::timestamp
            WHEN 'YESTERDAY'  THEN CURRENT_DATE::timestamp
            WHEN 'THIS_WEEK'  THEN (date_trunc('week',  CURRENT_DATE) + INTERVAL '7 days')::timestamp
            WHEN 'LAST_WEEK'  THEN date_trunc('week',  CURRENT_DATE)::timestamp
            WHEN 'THIS_MONTH' THEN (date_trunc('month', CURRENT_DATE) + INTERVAL '1 month')::timestamp
            WHEN 'LAST_MONTH' THEN date_trunc('month', CURRENT_DATE)::timestamp
        END AS end_ts
),
device_info AS (
    SELECT d.id AS device_id, d.name AS device_name, d.serial_number, d.alt_id, d.org_id
    FROM device d
    WHERE d.id = (SELECT dt_meter_id FROM params)
),
interval_metrics AS (
    SELECT
        di.device_id, COUNT(*) AS interval_count,
        ROUND(SUM(ir.kwh_import)::numeric,
        3) AS total_kwh_import,
        ROUND(SUM(ir.kwh_export)::numeric,
        3) AS total_kwh_export,
        ROUND(SUM(ir.kwh_net)::numeric,
        3) AS total_kwh_net,
        ROUND(SUM(ir.kvah)::numeric,
        3) AS total_kvah,
        ROUND((MAX(ir.kwh_import) * 12)::numeric,
        2) AS peak_kw,
        ROUND((AVG(ir.kwh_import) * 12)::numeric,
        2) AS avg_kw,
        ROUND((MAX(ir.kvah) * 12)::numeric,
        2) AS peak_kva,
        ROUND((AVG(ir.kvah) * 12)::numeric,
        2) AS avg_kva,
        ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN ir.kvah > 0 THEN ir.kwh_import / NULLIF(ir.kvah,
        0) END)::numeric,
        3) AS avg_power_factor,
        ROUND(AVG(ir.v_avg)::numeric,
        2) AS avg_voltage,
        ROUND(MIN(ir.v_avg)::numeric,
        2) AS min_voltage,
        ROUND(MAX(ir.v_avg)::numeric,
        2) AS max_voltage,
        ROUND(AVG(ir.i_avg)::numeric,
        2) AS avg_current,
        ROUND(MAX(ir.i_avg)::numeric,
        2) AS peak_current
    FROM device_info di
    JOIN interval_raw ir ON ir.device_id = di.device_id
    CROSS JOIN period p
    WHERE ir.interval_end_time >= p.start_ts AND ir.interval_end_time < p.end_ts
    GROUP BY di.device_id
),
peak_interval AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT ON (di.device_id)
        di.device_id, ir.interval_start_time AS peak_kw_start_time, ir.interval_end_time AS peak_kw_end_time,
        ROUND((ir.kwh_import * 12)::numeric,
        2) AS peak_kw_at_time,
        ROUND((ir.kvah * 12)::numeric,
        2) AS peak_kva_at_time,
        ROUND(ir.i_avg::numeric,
        2) AS current_at_peak, ROUND(ir.v_avg::numeric,
        2) AS voltage_at_peak
    FROM device_info di
    JOIN interval_raw ir ON ir.device_id = di.device_id
    CROSS JOIN period p
    WHERE ir.interval_end_time >= p.start_ts AND ir.interval_end_time < p.end_ts
    ORDER BY di.device_id, ir.kwh_import DESC NULLS LAST
),
daily_metrics AS (
    SELECT
        di.device_id, COUNT(DISTINCT dr.read_time::date) AS days_with_data,
        ROUND(SUM(dr.kwh_import)::numeric,
        3) AS daily_total_kwh_import,
        ROUND(SUM(dr.kwh_export)::numeric,
        3) AS daily_total_kwh_export,
        ROUND(MAX(dr.mdkw)::numeric,
        2) AS highest_md_kw,
        ROUND(MAX(dr.mdkva)::numeric,
        2) AS highest_md_kva,
        ROUND(AVG(dr.mdkw)::numeric,
        2) AS avg_daily_md_kw
    FROM device_info di
    JOIN daily_raw dr ON dr.device_id = di.device_id
    CROSS JOIN period p
    WHERE dr.read_time >= p.start_ts AND dr.read_time < p.end_ts
    GROUP BY di.device_id
),
events_enriched AS (
    SELECT
        di.device_id, er.event_time, er.event_reporting_time, er.event_code, em.description, em.severity,
        em.category, em.direction, em.pair_code, er.v_avg, er.ir AS i_r, er.iy AS i_y, er.ib AS i_b
    FROM device_info di
    JOIN event_raw er ON er.device_id = di.device_id
    JOIN event_master em ON em.event_code = er.event_code
    CROSS JOIN period p
    WHERE er.event_time >= p.start_ts AND er.event_time < p.end_ts
),
event_paired AS (
    SELECT
        occ.device_id, occ.event_code AS occurrence_code, occ.description, occ.severity, occ.category,
        occ.event_time AS occurrence_time, occ.v_avg AS voltage_at_event, occ.i_r, occ.i_y, occ.i_b,
        MIN(rst.event_time) AS restoration_time,
        ROUND(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (MIN(rst.event_time) - occ.event_time)) / 60.0,
        1) AS duration_minutes
    FROM events_enriched occ
    LEFT JOIN events_enriched rst ON rst.device_id = occ.device_id AND rst.event_code = occ.pair_code
          AND rst.direction = 'RESTORATION' AND rst.event_time > occ.event_time
    WHERE occ.direction = 'OCCURRENCE'
    GROUP BY occ.device_id, occ.event_code, occ.description, occ.severity, occ.category, occ.event_time,
             occ.v_avg, occ.i_r, occ.i_y, occ.i_b
    UNION ALL
    SELECT device_id, event_code, description, severity, category, event_time, v_avg, i_r, i_y, i_b, NULL, NULL
    FROM events_enriched WHERE direction = 'STANDALONE'
),
event_summary AS (
    SELECT
        device_id, category AS event_category, severity AS event_severity, COUNT(*) AS event_count,
        COUNT(restoration_time) AS resolved_count, COUNT(*) - COUNT(restoration_time) AS unresolved_count,
        ROUND(SUM(COALESCE(duration_minutes,
        0)),
        1) AS total_duration_minutes,
        ROUND(AVG(duration_minutes),
        1) AS avg_duration_minutes,
        ROUND(MAX(duration_minutes),
        1) AS max_duration_minutes
    FROM event_paired
    GROUP BY device_id, category, severity
)
SELECT * FROM (
    SELECT di.device_name AS device_name, di.serial_number AS serial_number, di.alt_id AS dt_meter_id,
           p.start_ts AS period_start, p.end_ts AS period_end, (SELECT period_type FROM params) AS period_type,
           'Device & Period' AS section, 'Reporting Period' AS metric_name, NULL::numeric AS numeric_value,
           (SELECT period_type FROM params) AS text_value, '' AS unit,
           'From ' || p.start_ts::text || ' to ' || p.end_ts::text AS context
    FROM device_info di CROSS JOIN period p
    UNION ALL
    SELECT di.device_name, di.serial_number, di.alt_id, p.start_ts, p.end_ts, (SELECT period_type FROM params),
           'Energy Summary', 'Total Import Energy', im.total_kwh_import, NULL, 'kWh', 'Total energy imported from grid during the period'
    FROM device_info di CROSS JOIN period p LEFT JOIN interval_metrics im ON im.device_id = di.device_id
    UNION ALL
    SELECT di.device_name, di.serial_number, di.alt_id, p.start_ts, p.end_ts, (SELECT period_type FROM params),
           'Energy Summary', 'Total Export Energy', im.total_kwh_export, NULL, 'kWh', 'Total energy exported to grid during the period'
    FROM device_info di CROSS JOIN period p LEFT JOIN interval_metrics im ON im.device_id = di.device_id
    UNION ALL
    SELECT di.device_name, di.serial_number, di.alt_id, p.start_ts, p.end_ts, (SELECT period_type FROM params),
           'Energy Summary', 'Net Energy', im.total_kwh_net, NULL, 'kWh', 'Net energy (import minus export)'
    FROM device_info di CROSS JOIN period p LEFT JOIN interval_metrics im ON im.device_id = di.device_id
    UNION ALL
    SELECT di.device_name, di.serial_number, di.alt_id, p.start_ts, p.end_ts, (SELECT period_type FROM params),
           'Load & Demand', 'Peak Demand (kW)', pi.peak_kw_at_time, NULL, 'kW', 'Highest 5-min demand recorded'
    FROM device_info di CROSS JOIN period p LEFT JOIN peak_interval pi ON pi.device_id = di.device_id
    UNION ALL
    SELECT di.device_name, di.serial_number, di.alt_id, p.start_ts, p.end_ts, (SELECT period_type FROM params),
           'Events â€” ' || es.event_category, es.event_severity || ' Severity â€” ' || es.event_category,
           es.event_count::numeric, NULL, 'events',
           es.event_count || ' event(s) | ' || es.resolved_count || ' resolved | ' || es.unresolved_count || ' unresolved'
    FROM device_info di CROSS JOIN period p JOIN event_summary es ON es.device_id = di.device_id
) AS summary
ORDER BY CASE section WHEN 'Device & Period' THEN 1 WHEN 'Energy Summary' THEN 2 WHEN 'Load & Demand' THEN 3 WHEN 'Voltage & Current' THEN 4 ELSE 5 END, section, metric_name;

question: Compare & provide summary for peak load, avg load DT meters (DT Meter IDâ€™s) in between 08:00 to 12:00 and 16:00 to 18:00 for last period(1 week/yesterday/today/month)
sql:
WITH params AS (
    SELECT ARRAY['dev__CRYT3093621','dev__CRYT3090775','dev__CRYT3149541','dev__CRYT3246732'
        ] AS dt_meter_ids, 'THIS_WEEK' AS period_type
),
time_windows (window_label, window_start, window_end) AS (
    VALUES
    ('Morning Peak  (08: 00â€“12: 00)', TIME '08: 00', TIME '12: 00'),
    ('Evening Peak  (16: 00â€“18: 00)', TIME '16: 00', TIME '18: 00')
),
period AS (
    SELECT
        CASE (SELECT period_type FROM params)
            WHEN 'TODAY'      THEN CURRENT_DATE::timestamp
            WHEN 'YESTERDAY'  THEN (CURRENT_DATE - 1)::timestamp
            WHEN 'THIS_WEEK'  THEN date_trunc('week',  CURRENT_DATE)::timestamp
            WHEN 'LAST_MONTH' THEN (date_trunc('month',CURRENT_DATE) - INTERVAL '1 month')::timestamp
        END AS start_ts,
        CASE (SELECT period_type FROM params)
            WHEN 'TODAY'      THEN (CURRENT_DATE + 1)::timestamp
            WHEN 'YESTERDAY'  THEN CURRENT_DATE::timestamp
            WHEN 'THIS_WEEK'  THEN (date_trunc('week',  CURRENT_DATE) + INTERVAL '7 days')::timestamp
            WHEN 'LAST_MONTH' THEN date_trunc('month', CURRENT_DATE)::timestamp
        END AS end_ts
),
device_list AS (
    SELECT d.id AS device_id, d.name AS device_name, d.serial_number, d.alt_id
    FROM device d
    WHERE d.id::text = ANY((SELECT dt_meter_ids FROM params)::text[])
),
windowed_intervals AS (
    SELECT
        dl.device_id, dl.device_name, dl.serial_number, dl.alt_id, tw.window_label, tw.window_start, tw.window_end,
        ir.interval_start_time, ir.interval_end_time, ir.kwh_import, ir.kwh_export, ir.kvah, ir.i_avg, ir.v_avg
    FROM device_list dl
    CROSS JOIN time_windows tw CROSS JOIN period p
    JOIN interval_raw ir ON ir.device_id = dl.device_id AND ir.interval_end_time >= p.start_ts AND ir.interval_end_time < p.end_ts
        AND ir.interval_end_time::time >= tw.window_start AND ir.interval_end_time::time < tw.window_end
),
window_metrics AS (
    SELECT
        device_id, device_name, serial_number, alt_id, window_label, window_start, window_end,
        COUNT(*) AS interval_count, ROUND((MAX(kwh_import) * 12)::numeric,
        2) AS peak_kw,
        ROUND((AVG(kwh_import) * 12)::numeric,
        2) AS avg_kw, ROUND((MAX(kvah) * 12)::numeric,
        2) AS peak_kva,
        ROUND((AVG(kvah) * 12)::numeric,
        2) AS avg_kva, ROUND(SUM(kwh_import)::numeric,
        3) AS total_kwh_import,
        ROUND(SUM(kwh_export)::numeric,
        3) AS total_kwh_export, ROUND(AVG(v_avg)::numeric,
        2) AS avg_voltage
    FROM windowed_intervals
    GROUP BY device_id, device_name, serial_number, alt_id, window_label, window_start, window_end
),
window_ranked AS (
    SELECT *,
        RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY window_label ORDER BY peak_kw DESC NULLS LAST) AS peak_kw_rank,
        RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY window_label ORDER BY avg_kw DESC NULLS LAST) AS avg_kw_rank
    FROM window_metrics
)
SELECT * FROM (
    SELECT wr.device_name, wr.serial_number, wr.alt_id AS dt_meter_id, p.start_ts AS period_start, p.end_ts AS period_end,
           (SELECT period_type FROM params) AS period_type, wr.window_label AS time_window, 'Summary' AS section,
           'Meter & Window Info' AS metric_name, NULL::numeric AS numeric_value, wr.device_name || ' | Window: ' || wr.window_label AS text_value,
           '' AS unit, wr.interval_count || ' intervals analysed | Ranked #' || wr.peak_kw_rank || ' for peak kW' AS context,
        1 AS sort_section, wr.peak_kw_rank AS sort_rank
    FROM window_ranked wr CROSS JOIN period p
    UNION ALL
    SELECT wr.device_name, wr.serial_number, wr.alt_id, p.start_ts, p.end_ts, (SELECT period_type FROM params),
           wr.window_label, 'Load', 'Peak Demand', wr.peak_kw, NULL, 'kW', 'Rank #' || wr.peak_kw_rank,
        2, wr.peak_kw_rank
    FROM window_ranked wr CROSS JOIN period p
    UNION ALL
    SELECT wr.device_name, wr.serial_number, wr.alt_id, p.start_ts, p.end_ts, (SELECT period_type FROM params),
           wr.window_label, 'Load', 'Average Demand', wr.avg_kw, NULL, 'kW', 'Rank #' || wr.avg_kw_rank,
        3, wr.avg_kw_rank
    FROM window_ranked wr CROSS JOIN period p
) AS summary
ORDER BY time_window, sort_section, sort_rank, device_name, metric_name;

question: Following query to know which device is at what level is feeder meter, dt meter or consumer meter
sql:
SELECT
    sp.id AS service_point_id, sp.name AS service_point_name, sp.alt_id AS service_point_alt_id,
    spc.type AS service_point_type, spc.name AS class_name,
    spdr.device_id, d.name AS device_name, d.serial_number, d.alt_id AS device_alt_id, d.device_type
FROM service_point sp
JOIN service_point_class spc ON spc.id = sp.service_point_class_id
LEFT JOIN service_point_device_rel spdr ON spdr.service_point_id = sp.id AND spdr.end_date IS NULL
LEFT JOIN device d ON d.id = spdr.device_id;

question: Give me summary of type of events occurred for past 1 week for DT meter/Feeder Meter for last 1 week period.
sql:
WITH params AS (
    SELECT 'Distribution Transformer' AS meter_type, 'dev__CRYT3120065' AS meter_id, 'THIS_WEEK' AS period_type
),
event_master (event_code, description, severity, category, direction, pair_code) AS (
    VALUES
    ('0003','Y-Phase VT link missing (Missing potential)','HIGH',  'Grid Reliability',   'OCCURRENCE','0004'),
    ('0004','Y-Phase VT link missing (Missing potential)','MEDIUM','Grid Reliability',   'RESTORATION','0003'),
    ('0101','Power Failure (3 Phase)',                    'HIGH',  'Grid Reliability',   'OCCURRENCE','0102')
),
period AS (
    SELECT
        CASE (SELECT period_type FROM params)
            WHEN 'THIS_WEEK'  THEN date_trunc('week',  CURRENT_DATE)::timestamp
        END AS start_ts,
        CASE (SELECT period_type FROM params)
            WHEN 'THIS_WEEK'  THEN (date_trunc('week',  CURRENT_DATE) + INTERVAL '7 days')::timestamp
        END AS end_ts
),
meter_list AS (
    SELECT d.id AS device_id, d.name AS device_name, d.serial_number, d.alt_id AS device_alt_id,
           spc.type AS meter_type, sp.name AS service_point_name
    FROM device d
    JOIN service_point_device_rel spdr ON spdr.device_id = d.id AND spdr.end_date IS NULL
    JOIN service_point sp ON sp.id = spdr.service_point_id
    JOIN service_point_class spc ON spc.id = sp.service_point_class_id
    WHERE d.id::text = (SELECT meter_id FROM params) AND spc.type::text = (SELECT meter_type FROM params)
),
events_enriched AS (
    SELECT ml.device_id, ml.device_name, ml.serial_number, ml.device_alt_id, ml.meter_type, ml.service_point_name,
           er.event_time, er.event_code, em.description, em.severity, em.category, em.direction, em.pair_code,
           er.v_avg, er.ir AS i_r, er.iy AS i_y, er.ib AS i_b
    FROM meter_list ml
    JOIN event_raw er ON er.device_id = ml.device_id
    JOIN event_master em ON em.event_code = er.event_code
    CROSS JOIN period p
    WHERE er.event_time >= p.start_ts AND er.event_time < p.end_ts
),
event_paired AS (
    SELECT occ.device_id, occ.device_name, occ.serial_number, occ.device_alt_id, occ.meter_type, occ.service_point_name,
           occ.event_code AS occurrence_code, occ.description, occ.severity, occ.category, occ.event_time AS occurrence_time,
           MIN(rst.event_time) AS restoration_time,
           ROUND(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (MIN(rst.event_time) - occ.event_time)) / 60.0,
        1) AS duration_minutes,
           CASE WHEN MIN(rst.event_time) IS NULL THEN 'Active / Unresolved' ELSE 'Resolved' END AS status
    FROM events_enriched occ
    LEFT JOIN events_enriched rst ON rst.device_id = occ.device_id AND rst.event_code = occ.pair_code
          AND rst.direction = 'RESTORATION' AND rst.event_time > occ.event_time
    WHERE occ.direction = 'OCCURRENCE'
    GROUP BY occ.device_id, occ.device_name, occ.serial_number, occ.device_alt_id, occ.meter_type, occ.service_point_name,
             occ.event_code, occ.description, occ.severity, occ.category, occ.event_time
    UNION ALL
    SELECT device_id, device_name, serial_number, device_alt_id, meter_type, service_point_name,
           event_code, description, severity, category, event_time, NULL, NULL, 'Standalone'
    FROM events_enriched WHERE direction = 'STANDALONE'
),
event_type_summary AS (
    SELECT device_id, device_name, serial_number, device_alt_id, meter_type, service_point_name, category,
           description AS event_type, severity, occurrence_code AS event_code, COUNT(*) AS total_occurrences,
           COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'Resolved') AS resolved_count,
           COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'Active / Unresolved') AS unresolved_count,
           ROUND(SUM(COALESCE(duration_minutes,
        0)),
        1) AS total_duration_minutes,
           ROUND(AVG(duration_minutes),
        1) AS avg_duration_minutes, ROUND(MAX(duration_minutes),
        1) AS max_duration_minutes,
           MIN(occurrence_time) AS first_occurrence, MAX(occurrence_time) AS last_occurrence
    FROM event_paired
    GROUP BY device_id, device_name, serial_number, device_alt_id, meter_type, service_point_name,
             category, description, severity, occurrence_code
),
category_rollup AS (
    SELECT device_id, category, SUM(total_occurrences) AS category_total, SUM(resolved_count) AS category_resolved,
           SUM(unresolved_count) AS category_unresolved, ROUND(SUM(total_duration_minutes),
        1) AS category_total_minutes,
           COUNT(DISTINCT event_type) AS distinct_event_types
    FROM event_type_summary GROUP BY device_id, category
)
SELECT * FROM (
    SELECT ets.device_name, ets.serial_number, ets.device_alt_id AS dt_meter_id, ets.meter_type, ets.service_point_name,
           p.start_ts AS period_start, p.end_ts AS period_end, (SELECT period_type FROM params) AS period_type,
           'Category Rollup' AS section, cr.category AS metric_name, cr.category_total::numeric AS numeric_value,
           NULL AS text_value, 'events' AS unit,
           cr.category_total || ' total event(s) in [' || cr.category || '
        ] | ' || cr.category_total_minutes || ' total minutes affected' AS context,
        1 AS sort_section, cr.category_total AS sort_val
    FROM category_rollup cr JOIN event_type_summary ets USING (device_id, category) CROSS JOIN period p
    GROUP BY ets.device_name, ets.serial_number, ets.device_alt_id, ets.meter_type, ets.service_point_name, p.start_ts, p.end_ts,
             cr.category, cr.category_total, cr.distinct_event_types, cr.category_resolved, cr.category_unresolved, cr.category_total_minutes
    UNION ALL
    SELECT ets.device_name, ets.serial_number, ets.device_alt_id, ets.meter_type, ets.service_point_name, p.start_ts, p.end_ts,
           (SELECT period_type FROM params), 'Event Type Detail' AS section, '[' || ets.severity || '
        ] ' || ets.event_type AS metric_name,
           ets.total_occurrences::numeric, NULL, 'occurrences',
           ets.total_occurrences || ' occurrence(s) | ' || ets.event_code || ' | ' || ets.severity || ' severity' AS context,
        2 AS sort_section, ets.total_occurrences AS sort_val
    FROM event_type_summary ets CROSS JOIN period p
) AS summary ORDER BY device_name, sort_section, sort_val DESC, metric_name;

question: Give me total counts & duration of event related to power outage for consumers in city Raipur.
sql:
WITH params AS (
    SELECT 'Raipur' AS city_search, 'THIS_WEEK' AS period_type
),
outage_events (event_code, description, direction, pair_code) AS (
    VALUES ('0101', 'Power Failure (3 Phase)', 'OCCURRENCE',  '0102'), ('0102', 'Power Failure (3 Phase)', 'RESTORATION', '0101')
),
period AS (
    SELECT
        CASE (SELECT period_type FROM params) WHEN 'THIS_WEEK' THEN date_trunc('week', CURRENT_DATE)::timestamp END AS start_ts,
        CASE (SELECT period_type FROM params) WHEN 'THIS_WEEK' THEN (date_trunc('week', CURRENT_DATE) + INTERVAL '7 days')::timestamp END AS end_ts
),
consumers_in_city AS (
    SELECT a.id AS address_id, a.city, a.district, a.state, a.pincode, sp.id AS service_point_id, sp.name AS service_point_name,
           d.id AS device_id, d.name AS device_name, d.serial_number, d.alt_id AS device_alt_id
    FROM address a
    JOIN service_point sp ON sp.address_id = a.id
    JOIN service_point_class spc ON spc.id = sp.service_point_class_id AND spc.type = 'Consumer'
    JOIN service_point_device_rel spdr ON spdr.service_point_id = sp.id AND spdr.end_date IS NULL
    JOIN device d ON d.id = spdr.device_id
    WHERE a.city ILIKE '%' || (SELECT city_search FROM params) || '%'
),
outage_raw AS (
    SELECT cc.device_id, cc.device_name, cc.serial_number, cc.device_alt_id, cc.service_point_id, cc.service_point_name,
           cc.city, cc.district, cc.state, cc.pincode, er.event_time, er.event_code, oe.description, oe.direction, oe.pair_code
    FROM consumers_in_city cc
    JOIN event_raw er ON er.device_id = cc.device_id
    JOIN outage_events oe ON oe.event_code = er.event_code
    CROSS JOIN period p
    WHERE er.event_time >= p.start_ts AND er.event_time < p.end_ts
),
outage_paired AS (
    SELECT occ.device_id, occ.device_name, occ.serial_number, occ.device_alt_id, occ.service_point_id, occ.service_point_name,
           occ.city, occ.district, occ.state, occ.pincode, occ.event_time AS outage_start, MIN(rst.event_time) AS outage_end,
           ROUND(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (MIN(rst.event_time) - occ.event_time)) / 60.0,
        1) AS outage_duration_minutes,
           CASE WHEN MIN(rst.event_time) IS NULL THEN 'Active / Unresolved' ELSE 'Resolved' END AS outage_status
    FROM outage_raw occ
    LEFT JOIN outage_raw rst ON rst.device_id = occ.device_id AND rst.event_code = occ.pair_code
          AND rst.direction = 'RESTORATION' AND rst.event_time > occ.event_time
    WHERE occ.direction = 'OCCURRENCE'
    GROUP BY occ.device_id, occ.device_name, occ.serial_number, occ.device_alt_id, occ.service_point_id, occ.service_point_name,
             occ.city, occ.district, occ.state, occ.pincode, occ.event_time
),
consumer_summary AS (
    SELECT device_id, device_name, serial_number, device_alt_id, service_point_name, city, district, state, pincode,
           COUNT(*) AS total_outages, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE outage_status = 'Resolved') AS resolved_outages,
           COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE outage_status = 'Active / Unresolved') AS active_outages,
           ROUND(SUM(COALESCE(outage_duration_minutes,
        0)),
        1) AS total_outage_minutes,
           ROUND(AVG(outage_duration_minutes),
        1) AS avg_outage_minutes, ROUND(MAX(outage_duration_minutes),
        1) AS max_outage_minutes,
           MIN(outage_start) AS first_outage_time, MAX(outage_start) AS last_outage_time
    FROM outage_paired
    GROUP BY device_id, device_name, serial_number, device_alt_id, service_point_name, city, district, state, pincode
),
city_rollup AS (
    SELECT city, district, state, COUNT(DISTINCT device_id) AS affected_consumer_count, SUM(total_outages) AS total_outages,
           SUM(resolved_outages) AS resolved_outages, SUM(active_outages) AS active_outages,
           ROUND(SUM(total_outage_minutes),
        1) AS total_outage_minutes, ROUND(AVG(avg_outage_minutes),
        1) AS avg_outage_minutes_per_consumer,
           ROUND(MAX(max_outage_minutes),
        1) AS worst_single_outage_minutes
    FROM consumer_summary
    GROUP BY city, district, state
)
SELECT cr.city, cr.district, cr.state, NULL AS serial_number, NULL AS consumer_device_id, NULL AS service_point_name,
       p.start_ts AS period_start, p.end_ts AS period_end, (SELECT period_type FROM params) AS period_type,
       (SELECT city_search FROM params) AS city_searched, 'City Rollup' AS section, 'Power Outage Overview â€” ' || cr.city AS metric_name,
       cr.total_outages::numeric AS numeric_value, NULL AS text_value, 'outages' AS unit,
       cr.affected_consumer_count || ' consumer meter(s) affected | ' || cr.total_outages || ' total outage(s)' AS context,
        1 AS sort_section, cr.total_outages AS sort_val
FROM city_rollup cr CROSS JOIN period p
UNION ALL
SELECT cs.city, cs.district, cs.state, cs.serial_number, cs.device_alt_id AS consumer_device_id, cs.service_point_name,
       p.start_ts, p.end_ts, (SELECT period_type FROM params), (SELECT city_search FROM params), 'Consumer Detail' AS section,
       cs.device_name AS metric_name, cs.total_outages::numeric, NULL, 'outages',
       cs.total_outages || ' outage(s) | Total: ' || cs.total_outage_minutes || ' min' AS context,
        2 AS sort_section, cs.total_outages AS sort_val
FROM consumer_summary cs CROSS JOIN period p
ORDER BY city, sort_section, sort_val DESC, metric_name;

question: How many events were related to voltage unbalance for past n days in DT/Feeder and tell me time of day for unbalance
sql:
WITH params AS (
    SELECT 'Distribution Transformer' AS meter_type, 'dev__CRYT3103559' AS meter_id,
        7 AS days_back
),
period AS (
    SELECT (CURRENT_DATE - (SELECT days_back FROM params))::timestamp AS start_ts, (CURRENT_DATE + 1)::timestamp AS end_ts
),
unbalance_events (event_code, description, category, direction, pair_code) AS (
    VALUES
    ('0063', 'Current unbalance', 'Current Unbalance','OCCURRENCE', '0064'),
    ('0064', 'Current unbalance', 'Current Unbalance','RESTORATION', '0063')
),
meter_info AS (
    SELECT d.id AS device_id, d.name AS device_name, d.serial_number, d.alt_id AS device_alt_id, spc.type AS meter_type, sp.name AS service_point_name
    FROM device d
    JOIN service_point_device_rel spdr ON spdr.device_id = d.id AND spdr.end_date IS NULL
    JOIN service_point sp ON sp.id = spdr.service_point_id
    JOIN service_point_class spc ON spc.id = sp.service_point_class_id
    WHERE d.id::text = (SELECT meter_id FROM params) AND spc.type::text = (SELECT meter_type FROM params)
),
events_raw AS (
    SELECT mi.device_id, mi.device_name, mi.serial_number, mi.device_alt_id, mi.meter_type, mi.service_point_name,
           er.event_time, er.event_code, ue.description, ue.category, ue.direction, ue.pair_code, er.v_avg, er.v_rn, er.v_yn, er.v_bn,
           EXTRACT(HOUR FROM er.event_time)::int AS event_hour,
           CASE
               WHEN EXTRACT(HOUR FROM er.event_time) BETWEEN 0 AND 5 THEN '1. Night       (00: 00â€“05: 59)'
               WHEN EXTRACT(HOUR FROM er.event_time) BETWEEN 6 AND 9 THEN '2. Early Morn  (06: 00â€“09: 59)'
               ELSE '6. Late Night  (22: 00â€“23: 59)'
           END AS time_slot, er.event_time::date AS event_date
    FROM meter_info mi
    JOIN event_raw er ON er.device_id = mi.device_id
    JOIN unbalance_events ue ON ue.event_code = er.event_code
    CROSS JOIN period p
    WHERE er.event_time >= p.start_ts AND er.event_time < p.end_ts
),
events_sequenced AS (
    SELECT *, LEAD(event_time) OVER (PARTITION BY device_id, event_code ORDER BY event_time) AS next_event_time,
           LEAD(event_code) OVER (PARTITION BY device_id, event_code ORDER BY event_time) AS next_event_code
    FROM events_raw
),
events_paired AS (
    SELECT device_id, device_name, serial_number, device_alt_id, meter_type, service_point_name, event_time AS occurrence_time,
           event_code, description, category, time_slot, event_hour, event_date, v_avg, v_rn, v_yn, v_bn,
           CASE WHEN next_event_code = pair_code THEN next_event_time END AS restoration_time,
           CASE WHEN next_event_code = pair_code THEN ROUND(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (next_event_time - event_time)) / 60.0,
        1) END AS duration_minutes,
           CASE WHEN next_event_code = pair_code THEN 'Resolved' ELSE 'Active / Unresolved' END AS status
    FROM events_sequenced WHERE direction = 'OCCURRENCE'
),
event_type_summary AS (
    SELECT device_id, category, description, event_code, COUNT(*) AS total_occurrences,
           COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'Resolved') AS resolved_count, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'Active / Unresolved') AS unresolved_count,
           ROUND(SUM(COALESCE(duration_minutes,
        0)),
        1) AS total_duration_minutes, ROUND(AVG(duration_minutes),
        1) AS avg_duration_minutes
    FROM events_paired GROUP BY device_id, category, description, event_code
),
time_of_day_summary AS (
    SELECT device_id, category, time_slot, COUNT(*) AS occurrence_count, ROUND(AVG(duration_minutes),
        1) AS avg_duration_minutes,
           ROUND(AVG(v_avg)::numeric,
        2) AS avg_voltage
    FROM events_paired WHERE v_rn IS NOT NULL AND v_yn IS NOT NULL AND v_bn IS NOT NULL GROUP BY device_id, category, time_slot
)
SELECT mi.device_name, mi.serial_number, mi.device_alt_id AS dt_meter_id, mi.meter_type, p.start_ts AS period_start, p.end_ts AS period_end,
       (SELECT days_back FROM params) || ' days' AS period_type, 'Event Count by Type' AS section,
       '[' || ets.category || '
        ] ' || ets.description AS metric_name, ets.total_occurrences::numeric AS numeric_value,
       NULL AS text_value, 'occurrences' AS unit, ets.total_occurrences || ' occurrence(s)' AS context,
        1 AS sort_section, ets.total_occurrences AS sort_val, NULL::text AS sort_date
FROM meter_info mi JOIN event_type_summary ets ON ets.device_id = mi.device_id CROSS JOIN period p
UNION ALL
SELECT mi.device_name, mi.serial_number, mi.device_alt_id, mi.meter_type, p.start_ts, p.end_ts, (SELECT days_back FROM params) || ' days',
       'Time of Day Hotspot', tod.time_slot, tod.occurrence_count::numeric, NULL, 'occurrences',
       tod.occurrence_count || ' occurrence(s) in ' || tod.time_slot,
        2, tod.occurrence_count, NULL::text
FROM meter_info mi JOIN time_of_day_summary tod ON tod.device_id = mi.device_id CROSS JOIN period p;

question: How many times voltage phase goes missing for DT/Feeder Meter at Raipur city?
sql:
WITH params AS (
    SELECT 'Feeder' AS meter_type, 'Raipur' AS city_search, 'THIS_WEEK' AS period_type
),
period AS (
    SELECT
        CASE (SELECT period_type FROM params) WHEN 'THIS_WEEK' THEN date_trunc('week', CURRENT_DATE)::timestamp END AS start_ts,
        CASE (SELECT period_type FROM params) WHEN 'THIS_WEEK' THEN (date_trunc('week', CURRENT_DATE) + INTERVAL '7 days')::timestamp END AS end_ts
),
matching_addresses AS (
    SELECT id AS address_id, city, district, state, pincode FROM address WHERE city ILIKE '%' || (SELECT city_search FROM params) || '%'
),
meters_in_city AS (
    SELECT d.id AS device_id, d.name AS device_name, d.serial_number, d.alt_id AS device_alt_id, spc.type AS meter_type,
           sp.id AS service_point_id, sp.name AS service_point_name, ma.city, ma.district, ma.state, ma.pincode
    FROM matching_addresses ma
    JOIN service_point sp ON sp.address_id = ma.address_id
    JOIN service_point_class spc ON spc.id = sp.service_point_class_id AND spc.type::text = (SELECT meter_type FROM params)
    JOIN service_point_device_rel spdr ON spdr.service_point_id = sp.id AND spdr.end_date IS NULL
    JOIN device d ON d.id = spdr.device_id
),
phase_events_raw AS (
    SELECT mc.device_id, mc.device_name, mc.serial_number, mc.device_alt_id, mc.meter_type, mc.service_point_name,
           mc.city, mc.district, mc.state, mc.pincode, er.event_time, er.event_code, er.v_avg, er.v_rn, er.v_yn, er.v_bn,
           CASE
               WHEN EXTRACT(HOUR FROM er.event_time) BETWEEN 0 AND 5 THEN '1. Night (00: 00â€“05: 59)'
               ELSE '6. Late Night (22: 00â€“23: 59)'
           END AS time_slot, er.event_time::date AS event_date
    FROM meters_in_city mc JOIN event_raw er ON er.device_id = mc.device_id CROSS JOIN period p
    WHERE er.event_code = '0003' AND er.event_time >= p.start_ts AND er.event_time < p.end_ts
),
phase_sequenced AS (
    SELECT mc.device_id, mc.device_name, mc.serial_number, mc.device_alt_id, mc.meter_type, mc.service_point_name,
           mc.city, mc.district, mc.state, mc.pincode, er.event_time, er.event_code, er.v_avg, er.v_rn, er.v_yn, er.v_bn,
           LEAD(er.event_time) OVER (PARTITION BY mc.device_id ORDER BY er.event_time) AS next_event_time,
           LEAD(er.event_code) OVER (PARTITION BY mc.device_id ORDER BY er.event_time) AS next_event_code
    FROM meters_in_city mc JOIN event_raw er ON er.device_id = mc.device_id CROSS JOIN period p
    WHERE er.event_code IN ('0003', '0004') AND er.event_time >= p.start_ts AND er.event_time < p.end_ts
),
phase_paired AS (
    SELECT device_id, device_name, serial_number, device_alt_id, meter_type, service_point_name, city, district, state, pincode,
           event_time AS occurrence_time, CASE WHEN next_event_code = '0004' THEN next_event_time END AS restoration_time,
           CASE WHEN next_event_code = '0004' THEN ROUND(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (next_event_time - event_time)) / 60.0,
        1) END AS duration_minutes,
           CASE WHEN next_event_code = '0004' THEN 'Resolved' ELSE 'Active / Unresolved' END AS status, v_avg, v_rn, v_yn, v_bn
    FROM phase_sequenced WHERE event_code = '0003'
),
meter_summary AS (
    SELECT device_id, device_name, serial_number, device_alt_id, meter_type, service_point_name, city, district, state, pincode,
           COUNT(*) AS total_occurrences, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'Resolved') AS resolved_count,
           COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'Active / Unresolved') AS unresolved_count,
           ROUND(SUM(COALESCE(duration_minutes,
        0)),
        1) AS total_duration_minutes, ROUND(AVG(duration_minutes),
        1) AS avg_duration_minutes,
           ROUND(MAX(duration_minutes),
        1) AS max_duration_minutes, ROUND(AVG(v_avg)::numeric,
        2) AS avg_voltage_at_event,
           MIN(occurrence_time) AS first_occurrence, MAX(occurrence_time) AS last_occurrence
    FROM phase_paired GROUP BY device_id, device_name, serial_number, device_alt_id, meter_type, service_point_name, city, district, state, pincode
),
city_rollup AS (
    SELECT city, district, state, COUNT(DISTINCT device_id) AS affected_meter_count, SUM(total_occurrences) AS total_occurrences,
           SUM(resolved_count) AS resolved_count, SUM(unresolved_count) AS unresolved_count,
           ROUND(SUM(total_duration_minutes),
        1) AS total_duration_minutes, ROUND(AVG(avg_duration_minutes),
        1) AS avg_duration_minutes,
           ROUND(MAX(max_duration_minutes),
        1) AS max_duration_minutes
    FROM meter_summary GROUP BY city, district, state
)
SELECT cr.city, cr.district, cr.state, NULL AS device_name, NULL AS serial_number, NULL AS dt_meter_id,
       (SELECT meter_type FROM params) AS meter_type, p.start_ts AS period_start, p.end_ts AS period_end,
       (SELECT period_type FROM params) AS period_type, (SELECT city_search FROM params) AS city_searched,
       'City Rollup' AS section, 'Y-Phase Missing Overview â€” ' || cr.city AS metric_name, cr.total_occurrences::numeric AS numeric_value,
       NULL AS text_value, 'occurrences' AS unit, cr.affected_meter_count || ' meter(s) affected | ' || cr.total_occurrences || ' total phase-missing event(s)' AS context,
        1 AS sort_section, cr.total_occurrences AS sort_val, NULL::text AS sort_date
FROM city_rollup cr CROSS JOIN period p
UNION ALL
SELECT ms.city, ms.district, ms.state, ms.device_name, ms.serial_number, ms.device_alt_id, ms.meter_type, p.start_ts, p.end_ts,
       (SELECT period_type FROM params), (SELECT city_search FROM params), 'Per Meter Detail', ms.device_name || ' (' || ms.service_point_name || ')',
       ms.total_occurrences::numeric, NULL, 'occurrences', ms.total_occurrences || ' phase-missing event(s)',
        2, ms.total_occurrences, NULL::text
FROM meter_summary ms CROSS JOIN period p;

question: Give me information for overall sanctioned load for consumer meters at City Birgaon.
sql:
WITH params AS (
    SELECT 'Birgaon' AS city_search
),
consumers_in_city AS (
    SELECT a.id AS address_id, a.city, a.district, a.state, a.pincode, sp.id AS service_point_id, sp.name AS service_point_name, sp.alt_id AS service_point_alt_id,
           d.id AS device_id, d.name AS device_name, d.serial_number, d.alt_id AS device_alt_id, sp.attr_json ->>'Load' AS raw_load,
           sp.attr_json ->>'Load Unit' AS load_unit, sp.attr_json ->>'IsGov' AS is_gov,
           CASE
               WHEN UPPER(TRIM(sp.attr_json ->>'Load Unit')) = 'W' THEN ROUND((sp.attr_json ->>'Load')::numeric / 1000.0,
        4)
               WHEN UPPER(TRIM(sp.attr_json ->>'Load Unit')) = 'KW' THEN ROUND((sp.attr_json ->>'Load')::numeric,
        4)
               WHEN UPPER(TRIM(sp.attr_json ->>'Load Unit')) IN ('HP', 'H.P') THEN ROUND((sp.attr_json ->>'Load')::numeric * 0.7457,
        4)
               ELSE NULL
           END AS load_kw
    FROM address a
    JOIN service_point sp ON sp.address_id = a.id
    JOIN service_point_class spc ON spc.id = sp.service_point_class_id AND spc.type = 'Consumer'
    JOIN service_point_device_rel spdr ON spdr.service_point_id = sp.id AND spdr.end_date IS NULL
    JOIN device d ON d.id = spdr.device_id
    WHERE a.city ILIKE '%' || (SELECT city_search FROM params) || '%' AND sp.attr_json ->>'Load' IS NOT NULL
),
city_rollup AS (
    SELECT city, district, state, COUNT(DISTINCT service_point_id) AS total_consumers,
           COUNT(DISTINCT service_point_id) FILTER (WHERE is_gov = 'Y') AS govt_consumers,
           COUNT(DISTINCT service_point_id) FILTER (WHERE is_gov = 'N' OR is_gov IS NULL) AS non_govt_consumers,
           COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE UPPER(TRIM(load_unit)) = 'W') AS load_in_watts_count,
           COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE UPPER(TRIM(load_unit)) = 'KW') AS load_in_kw_count,
           COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE UPPER(TRIM(load_unit)) IN ('HP','H.P')) AS load_in_hp_count,
           COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE load_kw IS NULL) AS unknown_unit_count, ROUND(SUM(load_kw),
        2) AS total_sanctioned_kw,
           ROUND(AVG(load_kw),
        2) AS avg_sanctioned_kw, ROUND(MIN(load_kw),
        2) AS min_sanctioned_kw, ROUND(MAX(load_kw),
        2) AS max_sanctioned_kw,
           ROUND(SUM(load_kw) FILTER (WHERE is_gov = 'Y'),
        2) AS total_govt_kw,
           ROUND(SUM(load_kw) FILTER (WHERE is_gov = 'N' OR is_gov IS NULL),
        2) AS total_non_govt_kw
    FROM consumers_in_city GROUP BY city, district, state
),
load_bands AS (
    SELECT city,
           CASE
               WHEN load_kw <= 1 THEN '1. Up to 1 kW'
               WHEN load_kw <= 5 THEN '2. 1 â€“ 5 kW'
               WHEN load_kw <= 10 THEN '3. 5 â€“ 10 kW'
               ELSE '7. Above 100 kW'
           END AS load_band,
           COUNT(*) AS consumer_count, ROUND(SUM(load_kw),
        2) AS band_total_kw, ROUND(AVG(load_kw),
        2) AS band_avg_kw
    FROM consumers_in_city WHERE load_kw IS NOT NULL GROUP BY city, load_band
)
SELECT cr.city, cr.district, cr.state, (SELECT city_search FROM params) AS city_searched, 'City Overview' AS section,
       'Sanctioned Load Overview â€” ' || cr.city AS metric_name, cr.total_sanctioned_kw AS numeric_value, NULL AS text_value, 'kW' AS unit,
       cr.total_consumers || ' consumer meter(s) | Total sanctioned load: ' || cr.total_sanctioned_kw || ' kW' AS context,
        1 AS sort_section, cr.total_sanctioned_kw AS sort_val, NULL::text AS sort_date
FROM city_rollup cr
UNION ALL
SELECT cr.city, cr.district, cr.state, (SELECT city_search FROM params), 'Govt vs Non-Govt', 'Government Consumers', cr.total_govt_kw, NULL, 'kW',
       cr.govt_consumers || ' govt consumer(s) | Total load: ' || COALESCE(cr.total_govt_kw::text, '0') || ' kW',
        2, cr.total_govt_kw, NULL::text
FROM city_rollup cr
UNION ALL
SELECT cr.city, cr.district, cr.state, (SELECT city_search FROM params), 'Govt vs Non-Govt', 'Non-Government Consumers', cr.total_non_govt_kw, NULL, 'kW',
       cr.non_govt_consumers || ' non-govt consumer(s) | Total load: ' || COALESCE(cr.total_non_govt_kw::text, '0') || ' kW',
        2, cr.total_non_govt_kw, NULL::text
FROM city_rollup cr
UNION ALL
SELECT lb.city, cr.district, cr.state, (SELECT city_search FROM params), 'Load Band Distribution', lb.load_band, lb.consumer_count::numeric, NULL, 'consumers',
       lb.consumer_count || ' consumer(s) in band ' || lb.load_band || ' | Total load: ' || lb.band_total_kw || ' kW',
        3, lb.consumer_count::numeric, lb.load_band
FROM load_bands lb JOIN city_rollup cr ON cr.city = lb.city
ORDER BY sort_section, sort_date ASC NULLS LAST, sort_val DESC NULLS LAST, metric_name;

question: What kind of business services are active on consumer meter EEOT3464414?
sql:
WITH params AS (
    SELECT 'EEOT3464414' AS meter_id
),
consumer_device AS (
    SELECT d.id AS device_id, d.name AS device_name, d.alt_id AS device_alt_id, d.serial_number, d.status AS device_status,
           spc.type AS meter_type, sp.id AS service_point_id, sp.name AS service_point_name, sp.alt_id AS service_point_alt_id
    FROM device d
    JOIN service_point_device_rel spdr ON spdr.device_id = d.id AND spdr.end_date IS NULL
    JOIN service_point sp ON sp.id = spdr.service_point_id
    JOIN service_point_class spc ON spc.id = sp.service_point_class_id AND spc.type = 'Consumer'
    WHERE d.alt_id::text = (SELECT meter_id FROM params)
),
active_services AS (
    SELECT cd.device_id, cd.device_name, cd.device_alt_id, cd.serial_number, cd.device_status, cd.meter_type, cd.service_point_name, cd.service_point_alt_id,
           bs.id AS business_service_id, bs.name AS business_service_name, bs.type AS business_service_type, bs.sub_type AS business_service_sub_type,
           bs.status AS business_service_status, bs.json_config AS service_config, ss.id AS stream_service_id, ss.start_date AS service_start_date,
           ss.end_date AS service_end_date, st.name AS stream_name, st.type AS stream_type, st.sub_type AS stream_sub_type
    FROM consumer_device cd
    JOIN stream_service ss ON ss.device_id = cd.device_id AND ss.end_date IS NULL
    JOIN business_service bs ON bs.id = ss.business_service_id AND bs.status = 'Active'
    JOIN stream s ON s.id = ss.stream_id
    JOIN stream_type st ON st.id = s.stream_type_id
)
SELECT * FROM (
    SELECT cd.device_alt_id AS meter_id, cd.device_name AS meter_name, cd.serial_number, cd.meter_type, cd.service_point_name, cd.service_point_alt_id,
           'Device Info' AS section, 'Consumer Meter' AS metric_name, NULL::numeric AS numeric_value, cd.device_alt_id AS text_value, '' AS unit,
           'Device: ' || cd.device_name || ' | Serial: ' || cd.serial_number || ' | Status: ' || cd.device_status AS context,
        1 AS sort_section,
        0::numeric AS sort_val
    FROM consumer_device cd
    UNION ALL
    SELECT asvc.device_alt_id, asvc.device_name, asvc.serial_number, asvc.meter_type, asvc.service_point_name, asvc.service_point_alt_id,
           'Active Business Services', asvc.business_service_name, NULL::numeric,
           asvc.business_service_type || COALESCE(' / ' || asvc.business_service_sub_type, ''), '',
           'Service: ' || asvc.business_service_name || ' | Type: ' || asvc.business_service_type AS context,
        2,
        0::numeric
    FROM active_services asvc
) AS summary ORDER BY sort_section, metric_name;

question: How many consumers are connected to DT (DT meter)
sql:
WITH params AS (
    SELECT 'CRYT3004552' AS dt_meter_id
),
dt_device AS (
    SELECT d.id AS device_id, d.name AS device_name, d.alt_id AS device_alt_id, d.serial_number, d.status AS device_status,
           sp.id AS service_point_id, sp.name AS service_point_name, sp.alt_id AS service_point_alt_id
    FROM device d
    JOIN service_point_device_rel spdr ON spdr.device_id = d.id AND spdr.end_date IS NULL
    JOIN service_point sp ON sp.id = spdr.service_point_id
    JOIN service_point_class spc ON spc.id = sp.service_point_class_id AND spc.type = 'Distribution Transformer'
    WHERE d.alt_id::text = (SELECT dt_meter_id FROM params)
),
consumer_service_point AS (
    SELECT dt.device_id AS dt_device_id, dt.device_name AS dt_device_name, dt.device_alt_id AS dt_device_alt_id, dt.serial_number AS dt_serial_number,
           dt.service_point_id AS dt_service_point_id, dt.service_point_name AS dt_service_point_name, sph.child_service_point_id AS consumer_sp_id
    FROM dt_device dt
    JOIN service_point_hierarchy_rel sph ON sph.parent_service_point_id = dt.service_point_id AND sph.end_date IS NULL
    JOIN service_point sp ON sp.id = sph.child_service_point_id
    JOIN service_point_class spc ON spc.id = sp.service_point_class_id AND spc.type = 'Consumer'
),
consumer_devices AS (
    SELECT csp.dt_device_id, csp.dt_device_name, csp.dt_device_alt_id, csp.dt_serial_number, csp.dt_service_point_id, csp.dt_service_point_name,
           csp.consumer_sp_id, sp.name AS consumer_sp_name, sp.alt_id AS consumer_sp_alt_id, d.id AS consumer_device_id, d.name AS consumer_device_name,
           d.alt_id AS consumer_device_alt_id, d.serial_number AS consumer_serial_number, d.status AS consumer_status
    FROM consumer_service_point csp
    JOIN service_point sp ON sp.id = csp.consumer_sp_id
    LEFT JOIN service_point_device_rel spdr ON spdr.service_point_id = csp.consumer_sp_id AND spdr.end_date IS NULL
    LEFT JOIN device d ON d.id = spdr.device_id
)
SELECT * FROM (
    SELECT cd.dt_device_alt_id AS dt_meter_id, cd.dt_device_name, cd.dt_serial_number, cd.dt_service_point_name,
           'DT Summary' AS section, 'Connected Consumers' AS metric_name, COUNT(DISTINCT cd.consumer_sp_id)::numeric AS numeric_value,
           NULL AS text_value, 'consumers' AS unit, 'DT Meter: ' || cd.dt_device_name || ' | Total connected consumer service points: ' || COUNT(DISTINCT cd.consumer_sp_id) AS context,
        1 AS sort_section, COUNT(DISTINCT cd.consumer_sp_id)::numeric AS sort_val
    FROM consumer_devices cd GROUP BY cd.dt_device_alt_id, cd.dt_device_name, cd.dt_serial_number, cd.dt_service_point_name
    UNION ALL
    SELECT cd.dt_device_alt_id, cd.dt_device_name, cd.dt_serial_number, cd.dt_service_point_name, 'Consumer Status Breakdown', cd.consumer_status,
           COUNT(*)::numeric, NULL, 'consumers', COALESCE(cd.consumer_status, 'No Device') || ': ' || COUNT(*) || ' consumer meter(s)',
        2, COUNT(*)::numeric
    FROM consumer_devices cd GROUP BY cd.dt_device_alt_id, cd.dt_device_name, cd.dt_serial_number, cd.dt_service_point_name, cd.consumer_status
    UNION ALL
    SELECT cd.dt_device_alt_id, cd.dt_device_name, cd.dt_serial_number, cd.dt_service_point_name, 'Consumer List',
           COALESCE(cd.consumer_device_name, cd.consumer_sp_name), NULL::numeric, COALESCE(cd.consumer_device_alt_id, cd.consumer_sp_alt_id), '',
           'Consumer SP: ' || cd.consumer_sp_name || ' | Status: ' || COALESCE(cd.consumer_status, 'N/A'),
        3, NULL::numeric
    FROM consumer_devices cd
) AS summary ORDER BY sort_section, sort_val DESC NULLS LAST, metric_name;

question: How many consumers are with single phase meter/three phase meter?
sql:
WITH params AS (
    SELECT NULL::text AS city_search, NULL::text AS org_id
),
phase_types (sub_type, phase_label, phase_description) AS (
    VALUES ('D1', 'Single Phase', '1-phase smart consumer meter'), ('D2', 'Three Phase', '3-phase smart consumer meter')
),
consumer_meters AS (
    SELECT d.id AS device_id, d.name AS device_name, d.alt_id AS device_alt_id, d.serial_number, d.status AS device_status, d.org_id,
           p.sub_type AS product_sub_type, p.name AS product_name, p.type AS product_type, pt.phase_label, pt.phase_description,
           sp.id AS service_point_id, sp.name AS service_point_name, spc.type AS meter_type, a.city, a.district, a.state
    FROM device d
    JOIN product p ON p.id = d.product_id
    JOIN phase_types pt ON pt.sub_type = p.sub_type
    JOIN service_point_device_rel spdr ON spdr.device_id = d.id AND spdr.end_date IS NULL
    JOIN service_point sp ON sp.id = spdr.service_point_id
    JOIN service_point_class spc ON spc.id = sp.service_point_class_id AND spc.type = 'Consumer'
    JOIN address a ON a.id = sp.address_id
    WHERE ((SELECT city_search FROM params) IS NULL OR a.city ILIKE '%' || (SELECT city_search FROM params) || '%')
      AND ((SELECT org_id FROM params) IS NULL OR d.org_id = (SELECT org_id FROM params))
),
overall_summary AS (
    SELECT phase_label, phase_description, product_sub_type, COUNT(*) AS total_meters,
           COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE device_status = 'Active') AS active_meters, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE device_status != 'Active') AS inactive_meters,
           COUNT(DISTINCT city) AS city_count, COUNT(DISTINCT org_id) AS org_count
    FROM consumer_meters GROUP BY phase_label, phase_description, product_sub_type
),
city_breakdown AS (
    SELECT city, district, state, phase_label, product_sub_type, COUNT(*) AS meter_count,
           COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE device_status = 'Active') AS active_count, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE device_status != 'Active') AS inactive_count
    FROM consumer_meters GROUP BY city, district, state, phase_label, product_sub_type
)
SELECT * FROM (
    SELECT COALESCE((SELECT city_search FROM params), 'All Cities') AS scope, 'Overall Summary' AS section,
           os.phase_label || ' Meter (' || os.product_sub_type || ')' AS metric_name, os.total_meters::numeric AS numeric_value,
           NULL AS text_value, 'meters' AS unit, os.phase_label || ' (' || os.product_sub_type || ') | Total: ' || os.total_meters || ' meters' AS context,
        1 AS sort_section, os.total_meters::numeric AS sort_val, NULL::text AS sort_date
    FROM overall_summary os
    UNION ALL
    SELECT COALESCE((SELECT city_search FROM params), 'All Cities'), 'Overall Summary', 'Total Smart Consumer Meters',
           SUM(total_meters)::numeric, NULL, 'meters', 'Grand total: ' || SUM(total_meters) || ' consumer smart meters',
        1, SUM(total_meters)::numeric, NULL::text
    FROM overall_summary
    UNION ALL
    SELECT cb.city || ', ' || cb.state, 'City Breakdown', cb.city || ' â€” ' || cb.phase_label || ' (' || cb.product_sub_type || ')',
           cb.meter_count::numeric, NULL, 'meters', 'City: ' || cb.city || ' | Total: ' || cb.meter_count,
        2, cb.meter_count::numeric, cb.city
    FROM city_breakdown cb
) AS summary ORDER BY sort_section, sort_date ASC NULLS LAST, sort_val DESC NULLS LAST, metric_name;
